description: From the Publisher Amazing results every time Younger children love our Trace-Along Scratch and Sketch Books. Just trace the outlines & see dinosaur pictures appear like magic in silver, gold, & swirls of color. Easy-to-trace white outlines Take a journey back in time to learn about 20 top dinosaurs: the smallest, the biggest, the cleverest, the speediest, the fiercest, and 15 more! Trace each picture's white outlines to reveal brilliant colors beneath the black-coated pages. Gold, silver, and colorful swirls! Metallic and rainbow colors appear like magic. Just use the wooden stylus provided. Draw your own sketches in the back of the book. Kids can use the 20 extra sketch pages in the back of the book to draw their own dino dudes and dudettes. Trace-Along fun lets kids build confidence in their creativity! This art activity book is packed with easy pictures to trace. Budding artists can use the wooden stylus enclosed to trace each outline and create each picture. They'll love the results. Black page coating is kid-safe and fun to scratch off. When you’re finished, your drawings will stand out in bright hues or sparkles. Hours of fun! Educational too! Scratch and Sketch Books provide a rewarding independent activity for young artists. And Dino Dudes is packed with fun facts about 20 of your fave dinosaurs, including Brontosaurus, Triceratops, Ichthyosaur, and of course -- Tyrannosaurus Rex. Great for travel Scratch and Sketch Books are also perfect for travel -- all you need is the Book and its wooden stylus for use in the car, the hotel, restaurants, and more. (Pack pencils or crayons for the extra sketching pages in the back.) About this Art Activity Book: Includes a wooden stylus to scratch the black-coated pages, revealing the amazing colors beneath! Sturdy wire-o hardcover binding 20 scratch-off black-coated pages 20 illustrated pages 20 sketch pages Recommended for ages 5 and up. Not for children under 5 years. Meets all applicable safety standards Book measures 6-3/8 inches wide x 8-1/2 inches high Non-toxic: Potential eye irritant. Avoid inhaling particles of scratch coating. Peter -- and later, his wife, Edna -- sought to create fine books that sold at prices even a pauper could afford. Today, still family owned and operated, Peter Pauper Press continues to honor our founders’ legacy -- and our customers’ expectations -- of beauty, quality, and value.
